plan_type_rationale: 7 ordered audit phases with light dependency chain. Each is a discrete verification/measurement task.

phases.name: source-headers — coverage delta measurement

tasks.description: Count current missing+stale headers: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const idx=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('.coderef/index.json')); const e=idx.elements||[]; console.log('missing:',e.filter(x=>x.headerStatus==='missing').length,'stale:',e.filter(x=>x.headerStatus==='stale').length,'defined:',e.filter(x=>x.headerStatus==='defined').length);"
Run populate-coderef --source-headers (fills gaps only, preserveExisting:true): node dist/src/cli/populate.js --source-headers
Re-scan to refresh index: node dist/src/cli/coderef-scan.js (or npm run scan if available)
Count missing+stale headers AFTER stamp — record before/after delta
Commit any newly-stamped headers: git add src/ && git commit -m 'feat(source-headers): phase 1 fresh-pass — fill remaining gaps'

acceptance_criteria: Before/after header counts recorded
Missing header count reduced or confirmed at floor
No hand-curated fields overwritten

phases.name: tree-sitter-verify — field extraction correctness

tasks.description: Run coderef-scan on a representative sample: node dist/src/cli/coderef-scan.js --verbose 2>&1 | head -50

Verify all element types are captured: interface_declaration, type_alias_declaration, function_declaration, class_declaration, variable_declaration. Check .coderef/index.json element type distribution.
Verify walkRawTsExports includes interface + type alias (committed in 6a28e62). Spot-check: grep for ConversionOptions in index.json — should have exported:true

Count element types: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const idx=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('.coderef/index.json')); const e=idx.elements||[]; const types={}; e.forEach(x=>{types[x.elementType]=(types[x.elementType]||0)+1;}); console.log(JSON.stringify(types,null,2));"

acceptance_criteria: interface_declaration and type_alias_declaration present in element type distribution
ConversionOptions correctly flagged exported:true (no false staleness)
No missing element types in a representative src/ file

phases.name: export-edges — relationship accuracy audit

tasks.description: Check export edge count in graph.json: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const g=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('.coderef/graph.json')); const edges=g.edges||[]; const exports=edges.filter(e=>e.type==='exports'); console.log('export edges:',exports.length,'total edges:',edges.length);"
Spot-check 3 files with known exports (e.g. graph-to-elements.ts, types.ts, utils/fs.ts): verify their exported symbols appear as edges in graph.json
Verify no duplicate export edges for the same symbol: count distinct source+target pairs

acceptance_criteria: Export edge count is non-zero and plausible (> 100 for this codebase)
Spot-checked files have correct export edges
No duplicate export edges detected

phases.name: what-imports-fix — import resolution audit

tasks.description: Run coderef-query --type=what-imports for a known file: node dist/src/cli/coderef-query.js --type=what-imports --file=src/pipeline/orchestrator.ts 2>&1 | head -30
Verify import edges resolve correctly — no broken relative paths, no missing importee elements

Check import edge count in graph.json: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const g=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('.coderef/graph.json')); const edges=g.edges||[]; console.log('import edges:',edges.filter(e=>e.type==='imports').length,'calls edges:',edges.filter(e=>e.type==='calls').length);"
If broken imports found: fix the scanner path normalization (Windows path separator issue from STUB-INDEXING-ORCHESTRATOR-PATH-NORMALIZATION-001 if still open)

acceptance_criteria: coderef-query --type=what-imports returns results for orchestrator.ts
Import edges count is non-zero
No broken relative path references in edge targets

phases.name: rag-search-constraint — constraint flag test

tasks.description: Test rag-search with --constraint flag: node dist/src/cli/rag-search.js --constraint=service 'orchestrator pipeline' 2>&1 | head -20
Test rag-search without constraint for comparison: node dist/src/cli/rag-search.js 'orchestrator pipeline' 2>&1 | head -20
Verify --constraint filters results to the specified layer (service layer elements only). Compare constrained vs unconstrained result sets.

Test via HTTP POST to RAG server: curl -s -X POST http://localhost:52849/api/rag/query -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"query":"export relationship scanner","constraint":"service","topK":5}' 2>&1

acceptance_criteria: --constraint flag reduces result set to matching layer
RAG server responds to constrained HTTP query
Constrained results differ from unconstrained (filter is active)

phases.name: viz-fields — visualization field output validation

tasks.description: Run graph export for visualization: node dist/src/cli/coderef-query.js --type=graph-export 2>&1 | head -30 (or equivalent viz command)
Verify graph nodes have required viz fields: id, label, layer, elementType. Check that edges have: source, target, type.

Check .coderef/graph.json node field coverage: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const g=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('.coderef/graph.json')); const nodes=g.nodes||[]; const sample=nodes.slice(0,3); console.log(JSON.stringify(sample,null,2));"

acceptance_criteria: Graph nodes contain id, label, layer, elementType fields
Graph edges contain source, target, type fields
No null/undefined required fields in sample

phases.name: ai-truncation — smart truncation behavior verification

tasks.description: Test context output with a large query: node dist/src/cli/rag-search.js --max-tokens=2000 'full pipeline orchestration export relationship staleness' 2>&1 | tail -10
Verify smart truncation kicks in for large result sets: output should be bounded, not overflow. Check that truncation preserves highest-scored results.
If --max-tokens flag not supported: test via agentic-context.json output size — verify it stays within bounds for a representative query

Commit .coderef/ snapshot and any minor fixes found during audit: git add .coderef/ && git commit -m 'audit(leverage-audit-fresh-pass): all 7 phases complete — header delta, tree-sitter, export-edges, imports, rag-constraint, viz-fields, ai-truncation'

acceptance_criteria: Truncation is active for large result sets (output bounded)
Highest-scored results preserved under truncation
All 7 audit phases recorded with findings
